title: Today's Markets Brief: Dow Jones Futures Waver, Snowflake Surges On Earnings; Fed Inflation Data Due
summary: A quick look at the day's market news, including Dow Jones futures, Snowflake's earnings surge, and FedEx's addition to the S&P 500.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Dow Jones Futures Waver, Snowflake Surges On Earnings; Fed Inflation Data Due
imageCaption: Dow Jones Futures Waver, Snowflake Surges On Earnings; Fed Inflation Data Due
imageSourceTitle: Dow Jones Futures Waver, Snowflake Surges On Earnings; Fed Inflation Data Due

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your markets brief. I am looking at a mixed bag of news today, with Dow Jones futures wavering and some big earnings moves. Let's dive in.

Dow Jones futures are wavering today, as investors await the Federal Reserve's inflation data due later this week. This data is closely watched, as it can influence the Fed's interest rate decisions. Meanwhile, Snowflake's stock is surging after the company reported strong earnings. The cloud data platform provider saw significant growth in its revenue, which is a positive sign for the company and the sector.

In other news, FedEx Freight Holding Company is set to join the S&P 500, replacing a current constituent. This move is seen as a positive development for FedEx, as it increases the company's visibility and liquidity. Additionally, EPAM Systems and Dave are joining the S&P SmallCap 600.

Snowflake's earnings report was a highlight today. The company's revenue growth was impressive, and its guidance for the next quarter was strong. This is a good sign for the company, which has been investing heavily in its platform and sales force. My read is that Snowflake's surge is a reflection of its solid execution and the growing demand for cloud-based data solutions.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
